summary_one_line: Input must carry a parseable tRNS chunk so png_ptr->num_trans is set non-zero, with PNG_EXPAND active.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A PNG whose chunk stream includes a valid tRNS chunk (FOURCC 0x74 0x52 0x4e 0x53 = 'tRNS') that png_handle_tRNS parses, setting png_ptr->num_trans != 0, combined with an expand transformation so (png_ptr->transformations & PNG_EXPAND) != 0 — making the compound condition at L4533/L4534 true.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Winner cmplog seeds drive png_handle_tRNS 10 times (W hits) vs 0 in the loser, and the blocker L4533 evaluates true in all 10 winner trials (T=10 F=0); the upstream num_trans checks L4459 and L4477 also take their true sides only in the winner. The winner byte sets at the chunk-FOURCC offsets 0x25-0x28 carry non-default values (e.g. 69/54/58/74) showing chunk-type bytes were mutated toward real ancillary chunks.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Loser naive seeds never invoke png_handle_tRNS (L hits = 0), so num_trans stays zero, and the blocker L4533 evaluates false in all 10 loser trials (T=0 F=10); at the FOURCC offsets 0x25-0x28 every loser seed keeps the default 67 41 4d 41 ('gAMA') bytes with no tRNS variant.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog's I2SRandReplace intercepts the FOURCC comparison in png_read_chunk dispatch, reads the expected 'tRNS' literal from the CmpLog buffer, and substitutes it into input bytes at the chunk-type offset, manufacturing a parseable tRNS chunk that sets num_trans non-zero; naive must hit the exact 4-byte FOURCC by blind havoc and never does in 10 trials.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the num_trans!=0 condition at L4533, requiring num_trans non-zero (with PNG_EXPAND).
The full compound gate also requires PNG_EXPAND set on the next source line.
The winner takes the blocker true side in all 10 trials while the loser takes false in all 10.
png_handle_tRNS fires 10 times in the winner set and 0 times in the loser set, so num_trans is set only in the winner.
Upstream num_trans check L4477 takes its true side only in the winner.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L4477  T=8 F=0  T=0 F=0  if (png_ptr->num_trans != 0)

evidence_trail.claim: Winner seeds carry mutated chunk-FOURCC bytes at offsets 0x25-0x28 while losers keep the default gAMA bytes.
cmplog adds I2SRandReplace, which scans the input for one CMP operand and substitutes the other operand at matching offsets.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets

evidence_trail.claim: naive has no I2S and relies on blind havoc/token byte mutation.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: **Observed `mutation_op` in seed metadata**: any of the above. No I2S.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: ran `python3 tools/db_query.py lineage --branch 4082 --role W --fuzzer cmplog --trial 1 --seed 230924cbdaa2cc5c` and observed an I2S-floor dash row (mutation_op = -) at depth 3 of the ancestor chain (tool reported '1 ancestor dash row(s) at depth 3'), the lower-bound signal of an unwrapped I2SRandReplace find under the current build.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: If a naive-only campaign reaches the L4533 true side at the same rate as cmplog within the budget (showing blind havoc can place the 4-byte tRNS FOURCC unaided), the I2S attribution is wrong.

weakest_evidence_point: The byte-diff evidence ties winner seeds to mutated chunk-FOURCC bytes but does not directly prove the substituted literal is exactly 'tRNS' rather than another expand-triggering ancillary chunk.
